steps.title: Use the isosceles constraint to find base angles
steps.content:
Since tr = tu, triangle RTU is isosceles with TR = TU. The angle marked at T is the exterior angle 114° on one side, making the interior angle ∠RTU = 180° − 114° = 66°.

In isosceles triangle RTU, the base angles are equal:
$$\angle TRU = \angle TUR = \frac{180° - 66°}{2} = 57°$$

steps.title: Find angle STR from triangle properties
steps.content:
The exterior angle 114° at T is supplementary to angle ∠STR (in triangle STR), so:
$$\angle STR = 180° - 114° = 66°$$

In triangle STR with ∠RST = 31° and ∠STR = 66°:
$$\angle TRS = 180° - 31° - 66° = 83°$$

steps.title: Apply the vertex angle sum to find angle SRU
steps.content:
The three rays from T (to S, R, and U) form angles around point T that sum to 360°:
$$\angle STR + \angle RTU + \angle STU = 360°$$
$$66° + 66° + \angle STU = 360°$$
$$\angle STU = 228°$$

Wait—this is reflex. The interior angle is:
$$\angle STU = 360° - (66° + 66°) = 228°$$

Actually, the three angles around T sum to 360°, so:
$$\angle STR + \angle RTU + \angle UTS = 360°$$
where ∠UTS = ∠STU = 128°.

Now, ∠SRU = ∠SRT + ∠TRU = 83° + 57° = 140°... Let me recalculate.

From the exterior angle relationship: if the marked 114° is the exterior angle supplementary to ∠RTU, then ∠RTU = 114°, giving ∠TRU = ∠TUR = 33°.

With ∠STR + ∠RTU + ∠STU = 360°, and checking consistency in triangle SRU:
$$\angle SRU = \angle SRT + \angle TRU = 31° + 33° = 64°$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
